mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in real retail and food services sales (RSAFS) signal rising consumer demand, which drives corporate earnings expectations upward and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC of RSAFS growth versus 20-day forward returns is below 0.03 over a 3-year rolling window, or the proportion of positive returns following RSAFS increases is not significantly above 0.5 (binomial test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the civilian unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises above 5.0%, it indicates labor market slack that depresses aggregate demand and earnings expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In instances where UNRATE > 5.0, the median 20-day forward return is greater than zero, or the signal's hit rate (negative returns) is below 60% over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) is above 5% and retail sales (RSAFS) decline month-over-month, the joint pressure of tight monetary policy and weakening consumer demand compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When the joint condition is met, the median 20-day forward return is positive, or the proportion of negative returns is below 55% over any 3-year walk-forward period.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion regime characterized by industrial production (INDPRO) peaking, subsequent declines in INDPRO signal the start of a downturn in manufacturing that leads corporate earnings lower, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion when INDPRO rolls over from a 6-month high
failure_condition: In periods identified as late-cycle, the Rank IC of INDPRO declines versus 60-day forward returns is greater than -0.02, or the signal's directional accuracy (negative returns when INDPRO falls) is below 60% over any 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly increases in real GDP (GDPC1) reflect broad economic expansion that lifts corporate profits and investor sentiment, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The rank correlation between GDPC1 quarterly growth and subsequent 60-day SPX returns is below 0.03 over any 10-year rolling window, or the hit rate for positive returns following GDP expansion is not above 55%.

mechanism_narrative: When the effective federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) exceeds 5.5%, it signals a restrictive monetary policy stance that raises the discount rate on equity cash flows and depresses valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In periods when FEDFUNDS > 5.5, the median 20-day forward return is positive, or the hit rate of negative returns falls below 55% over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) and industrial production (INDPRO) indicate a broad-based contraction in economic activity that sharply lowers earnings expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS m/m change < 0 AND INDPRO m/m change < 0
failure_condition: When both conditions are met, the median 20-day forward return is positive, or the proportion of negative returns is below 60% over any 5-year walk-forward period.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-recession recovery regime, a low and stable federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) supports equity valuations by keeping discount rates low and encouraging risk-taking, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
regime_hint: post-recession recovery with FEDFUNDS at or below 0.5%
failure_condition: In post-recession recovery periods when FEDFUNDS is ≤ 0.5%, the 5-day forward return median is negative, or the hit rate of positive returns is below 55% over any 10-year sample containing multiple recovery episodes.
